mnemonics: DEAF InterFACE: Default methods Evolve APIs without breaking Functionality. They Allow Interfaces to Contain an Implementation, providing a Fallback.
keyPoints: Default methods allow you to add new methods to an interface without breaking existing implementations.
They are defined using the 'default' keyword and provide a method body.
Classes that implement the interface can use the default implementation or override it with their own.
commonMistakes: Assuming default methods can access instance state - they can only call other interface methods.
Attempting to define a default method in a class instead of an interface.
Forgetting that a class's implementation always takes precedence over a default method from an interface.
